seoTitle: SUACE Paraguay: Company Residency Route | from $70K
seoDescription: Gain Paraguay residency by forming a company via SUACE from USD 70,000. How the business route works, steps and requirements. Speak to Lincoln.

faqs.question: What is SUACE?
faqs.answer: SUACE is Paraguay's Unified System for Opening and Closing Companies (Sistema Unificado de Apertura y Cierre de Empresas) — a one-stop process that streamlines company formation. The route involves establishing and capitalising a Paraguayan company through this system.

faqs.question: How much do I need?
faqs.answer: The SUACE company route requires establishing and capitalising a company with at least USD 70,000.

faqs.question: Do I have to actively run the company?
faqs.answer: The route is built for operators who want a genuine, active business with real economic substance. The company should reflect real activity rather than exist only on paper — which is exactly how we structure it.

faqs.question: What can the company do?
faqs.answer: That depends on your plans. We design the company around legitimate activity that fits both your commercial objectives and the substance the route calls for.

faqs.question: Does it lead to the same citizenship pathway?
faqs.answer: Yes. The SUACE route feeds into the same Investor Pass framework — Foreign Investor Certificate, permanent residency, and eligibility to naturalise after the constitutional residence period.

blocks.h1: Paraguay residency through SUACE company formation.
blocks.lead: Establish and capitalise a Paraguayan company through SUACE — the unified one-stop system — and earn your residency by building a real business in the Southern Cone.
blocks.bgAlt: Modern business district — SUACE company formation in Paraguay

blocks.heading: For people who build, not just hold.

paragraphs.text: SUACE — the Sistema Unificado de Apertura y Cierre de Empresas, Paraguay's Unified System for Opening and Closing Companies — is the one-stop process that makes incorporating in Paraguay genuinely fast. The SUACE route qualifies you for the Investor Pass by establishing and capitalising a Paraguayan company with at least USD 70,000.

This is the operator's route. You end up with an active business, real economic substance on the ground, and a territorial tax footing to build from — the cleanest foundation for anyone who wants to actually do something in the region, not just qualify for a certificate.

asideNotice.title: Substance matters
asideNotice.body: The company should reflect genuine activity, not exist only on paper. We structure it to stand up — commercially and to the authorities.

blocks.imageAlt: Entrepreneurs in a modern Paraguay office

blocks.heading: A business and a base, in one move.

cards.title: Lowest active route
cards.text: At USD 70,000 it is the most capital-efficient way to qualify while ending up with something you control.

cards.title: Real substance
cards.text: An operating company is the strongest form of economic presence — useful far beyond the residency itself.

cards.title: Territorial footing
cards.text: A Paraguayan company sits inside a territorial system — efficient when structured correctly within your wider plan.

cards.title: Fast formation
cards.text: SUACE is purpose-built to streamline incorporation, consolidating the steps into a single coordinated process.

cards.title: Mercosur access
cards.text: A company anchored in Paraguay sits at the centre of the Mercosur bloc — a launchpad, not a cul-de-sac.

cards.title: Same destination
cards.text: The SUACE route leads to the identical residency and citizenship pathway as every other route.

paragraphs.text: The SUACE route turns company formation into your residency engine: build the business properly, capitalise it to threshold, then move through the certificate, residency, and, in time, naturalisation.

notice.title: One honest caveat
notice.body: The five-business-day figure applies only to the Foreign Investor Certificate. Permanent residency is a separate process with its own timeline.

items.title: Strategy & structuring
items.text: We confirm the SUACE route fits and design the company so it carries real substance.

items.title: Formation via SUACE
items.text: The Paraguayan company is incorporated through the unified one-stop system.

items.title: Capitalisation
items.text: The company is capitalised to meet the USD 70,000 qualifying threshold and documented.

items.title: Foreign Investor Certificate
items.text: With the file complete, the MIC issues the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) within five business days.

items.title: Permanent residency
items.text: We progress the permanent residency process and your Paraguayan cédula.

items.title: Toward citizenship
items.text: You maintain residence and your company, building toward naturalisation eligibility after three years.

blocks.pull: A company. A footing. A residency you operate.
blocks.bgAlt: Asunción waterfront skyline at dusk, Paraguay
